0.original: Everyone I talked to said, in effect, I'd love that.
0.b1: Everyone I talked to said they would really like that idea.
0.cn: 我交谈过的每个人都说，他们非常喜欢这个主意。

1.original: I continued to think about it and to figure how I could turn the idea into profit.
1.b1: I kept thinking about it and tried to find a way to make money from it.
1.cn: 我继续思考这件事，并设法找出如何将这个想法转化为利润。

2.original: Then one day, as I was driving to work, the answer came out of nowhere.
2.b1: Then one day, while I was driving to work, I suddenly got the answer.
2.cn: 后来有一天，我在开车去上班的路上，答案突然浮现在脑海中。

3.original: Why not buy a farm and divide it into estates?
3.b1: Why not buy a farm and split it into smaller pieces of land?
3.cn: 为什么不买下一个农场，然后把它分成若干地块呢？

4.original: I figured the land might be worth more in relatively small pieces than in one big piece.
4.b1: I thought the land would be worth more if I sold it in small pieces instead of one big piece.
4.cn: 我想，如果把土地分成小块出售，可能比整块出售更值钱。

5.original: Twenty-two miles from the center of the city, I found a worn-out fifty-acre farm priced at $8,500.
5.b1: I found an old 50-acre farm 22 miles from the city center that cost $8,500.
5.cn: 在离市中心22英里远的地方，我发现了一个破旧的50英亩农场，售价为8500美元。

6.original: I bought it, paying only one-third down and working out a mortgage with the owner for the balance.
6.b1: I bought it by paying one-third of the price and getting a loan for the rest.
6.cn: 我买下了它，只付了三分之一的首付，其余的钱则与房主商定通过抵押贷款支付。

7.original: Next, I planted pine seedlings where there were no trees.
7.b1: Next, I planted pine trees in the areas where there were no trees.
7.cn: 接下来，我在没有树的地方种上了松树苗。

8.original: I did this because a real estate man, whom I feel knows his business,
8.b1: I did this because a real estate expert, who I think knows his job well,
8.cn: 我这样做是因为一位房地产专家——我认为他很精通业务——

9.original: told me, people want trees these days, lots of trees.
9.b1: told me that people want to buy land with many trees these days.
9.cn: 告诉我，现在人们都想要有很多树的土地。

10.original: I wanted my prospective buyers to see that in a few years, their estate would be covered with beautiful pine trees.
10.b1: I wanted buyers to see that their land would soon be covered with beautiful pine trees.
10.cn: 我想让未来的买家看到，几年后他们的土地上将长满美丽的松树。

11.original: Then I got a surveyor to divide the 50 acres into 10 5-acre plots.
11.b1: Then I hired a surveyor to divide the 50 acres into 10 smaller plots of 5 acres each.
11.cn: 然后我请了一位测量员，把这50英亩土地分成了10块，每块5英亩。

12.original: Now I was ready to start selling.
12.b1: Now I was ready to start selling the land.
12.cn: 现在我准备开始销售了。

13.original: I got several mailing lists of young executives in the city and put on a small-scale direct mail campaign.
13.b1: I got lists of young professionals in the city and sent them information by mail.
13.cn: 我找来了一些城市年轻管理人员的名单，并开展了一次小规模的直邮宣传活动。

14.original: I pointed out how for only $3,000, the price of a small city lot, they could buy an estate.
14.b1: I explained that for $3,000, the price of a small city lot, they could buy a large piece of land.
14.cn: 我指出，只需3000美元——也就是城市里一小块地皮的价格——他们就能买到一块地。

15.original: I also described the potentials for recreation and wholesome living.
15.b1: I also described how they could enjoy nature and a healthy life there.
15.cn: 我还描述了在那里进行休闲活动和过健康生活的潜力。

16.original: In six weeks' time, working only evenings and on weekends, I sold all ten plots.
16.b1: In six weeks, by working only in the evenings and on weekends, I sold all ten plots.
16.cn: 在六周的时间里，我只利用晚上和周末工作，就卖掉了全部十块地。

18.original: Total costs, including the land, advertising, surveying and legal expenses, $10,400.
18.b1: The total costs, including the land, ads, and legal fees, were $10,400.
18.cn: 总成本，包括土地、广告、测量和法律费用，为10400美元。

20.original: I made a nice profit because I let myself be exposed to ideas of other intelligent people.
20.b1: I made a good profit because I listened to ideas from other smart people.
20.cn: 我赚到了不错的利润，因为我让自己接触到了其他聪明人的想法。

21.original: Had I not accepted that invitation to attend a luncheon with a group completely foreign to my occupational interests,
21.b1: If I hadn't gone to that lunch meeting with people outside my own field,
21.cn: 如果我没有接受邀请去参加那个午餐会，与一群在职业兴趣上与我完全不同的人交流，

22.original: my brain would have never worked out this successful plan for making a profit.
22.b1: I would never have found this successful way to make money.
22.cn: 我的大脑就永远不会想出这个赚钱的成功计划。

23.original: There are many ways to get mental stimulation, but here are two that you can incorporate into your pattern of life.
23.b1: There are many ways to get new ideas, and here are two you can use in your life.
23.cn: 获得思维启发的方法有很多，这里有两个你可以融入生活模式的方法。

24.original: First, join and meet regularly with at least one professional group that provides stimulation in your own occupational area.
24.b1: First, join a professional group in your field and meet with them regularly.
24.cn: 第一，加入至少一个专业团体并定期聚会，这能为你所在的职业领域提供启发。

25.original: Rub shoulders and minds with other success-oriented people.
25.b1: Talk and share ideas with other successful people.
25.cn: 与那些追求成功的人交流思想，碰撞火花。

26.original: So often I hear someone say, I picked up a great idea this noon at the blank meeting,
26.b1: I often hear people say, I got a great idea at the meeting today,
26.cn: 我经常听到有人说，我今天中午在会议上得到了一个好主意，

27.original: or during the meeting yesterday I got to thinking.
27.b1: or I started thinking during the meeting yesterday.
27.cn: 或者说，在昨天的会议期间，我产生了一些思考。

28.original: Remember, a mind that feeds only on itself soon is undernourished, becoming weak and incapable of creative progressive thought.
28.b1: Remember, a mind that does not learn from others will become weak and unable to think creatively.
28.cn: 记住，一个只靠自己思考的大脑很快会营养不良，变得虚弱，无法进行创造性的进步思维。

29.original: Stimulation from others is excellent mind food.
29.b1: Getting ideas from others is great food for your brain.
29.cn: 从他人那里获得的启发是极好的精神食粮。

30.original: Second, join and participate in at least one group outside your occupational interests.
30.b1: Second, join a group that is not related to your job.
30.cn: 第二，加入并参与至少一个与你职业兴趣无关的团体。

31.original: Association with people who have different job interests broadens your thinking and helps you to see the big picture.
31.b1: Meeting people with different jobs helps you think more broadly and see the big picture.
31.cn: 与拥有不同职业兴趣的人交往，能拓宽你的思维，帮助你从全局看问题。

32.original: You'll be surprised how mixing regularly with people outside your occupational area will stimulate your on-the-job thinking.
32.b1: You will be surprised how meeting people outside your job helps your work thinking.
32.cn: 你会惊讶地发现，经常与职业领域之外的人交流，会如何激发你在工作中的思考。
